invariants.assertion_hint: For any valid nitrosamine SMILES, assert 1 <= classify_cpca(smiles).category <= 5; test across all branches (forced Category 5, flowchart paths, all ring sizes, both authorities).
invariants.description: The computed category must always be an integer in the set {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}, corresponding to potency tiers from highest (1) to lowest (5) carcinogenic potency.
invariants.name: Category is always in valid range [1,5]

invariants.assertion_hint: For all valid categories, verify _AI_LIMITS[1] < _AI_LIMITS[2] < _AI_LIMITS[3] < _AI_LIMITS[4], and _AI_LIMITS[4] == _AI_LIMITS[5]. For any SMILES, assert result.ai_limit_ng_per_day == _AI_LIMITS[result.category] or _AI_LIMIT_CAT1_EMA when authority=='EMA' and category==1.
invariants.description: As category increases (lower potency), the AI limit should increase or stay equal: _AI_LIMITS[cat] >= _AI_LIMITS[cat+1] is false; actually AI limits go 26.5 (cat 1) → 100 (cat 2) → 400 (cat 3) → 1500 (cat 4&5). Inverse: cat 1 < cat 2 < cat 3 = cat 4 in terms of allowed dose.
invariants.name: AI limit is strictly monotonic inverse to category

invariants.assertion_hint: For any list of nitrosamines where sum(measured_ng_per_day / ai_limit) == 1.0, verify calculate_cumulative_risk() returns passes=False. Repeat with sum slightly below 1.0 (e.g. 0.9999) and verify passes=True.
invariants.description: The FDA Rev 2 cumulative-risk rule requires total_risk_ratio < 1.0, not <= 1.0. A component exactly at its AI limit (measured == ai_limit_ng_per_day, ratio == 1.0) alone fails; multiple components summing to exactly 1.0 also fail.
invariants.name: Cumulative risk threshold is strict inequality (< 1.0, not <= 1.0)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: CPCA alpha-hydrogen score table (_ALPHA_H_SCORE['3,3']=1) and AI limits (_AI_LIMITS[1]=26.5)
worked_examples.name: NDMA Category 1 (FDA)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: EMA-specific Category 1 AI limit (_AI_LIMIT_CAT1_EMA=18.0)
worked_examples.name: NDMA Category 1 (EMA)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: CPCA alpha-hydrogen score table (_ALPHA_H_SCORE['2,2']=1)
worked_examples.name: NDEA Category 1

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Flowchart: alpha_h_score (1) + morpholine ring feature (1) = score 2 → Category 2; AI limits (_AI_LIMITS[2]=100.0)
worked_examples.name: N-nitrosomorpholine Category 2

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Flowchart: alpha_h_score (1) + 6-ring feature (2) = score 3 → Category 3; AI limits (_AI_LIMITS[3]=400.0)
worked_examples.name: N-nitrosopiperidine Category 3

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Flowchart: alpha_h_score (1) + pyrrolidine ring feature (3) = score 4 → Category 4; AI limits (_AI_LIMITS[4]=1500.0)
worked_examples.name: N-nitrosopyrrolidine Category 4

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Forced Category 5 rule: tertiary alpha-carbon detected (no potency_score computed); AI limits (_AI_LIMITS[5]=1500.0)
worked_examples.name: Di-tert-butyl nitrosamine Forced Category 5 (tertiary alpha)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Forced Category 5 rule: alpha_h_score (1,1) = 4 (sentinel value forces Category 5); AI limits (_AI_LIMITS[5]=1500.0)
worked_examples.name: Diisopropyl nitrosamine Forced Category 5 (low alpha-H)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Flowchart: alpha_h_score (2,3)=1 + carboxylic_acid feature (3) = score 4 → Category 4; feature scores (_FEATURE_SCORES['carboxylic_acid']=3)
worked_examples.name: N-nitroso-sarcosine Category 4 (carboxylic acid)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Flowchart: alpha_h_score (2,3)=1 + beta-hydroxyl one side (1) = score 2 → Category 2; feature scores (_FEATURE_SCORES['beta_hydroxyl_one_side']=1)
worked_examples.name: N-nitroso-2-aminoethanol Category 2 (beta-hydroxyl)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Feature scores: benzylic activating feature (_FEATURE_SCORES['aryl_on_alpha_benzylic']=-1)
worked_examples.name: NMBzA benzylic activating

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Cumulative risk rule: sum(measured / AI_limit) < 1.0 (FDA Rev 2); (10/26.5 + 10/26.5) ≈ 0.755 < 1.0 → passes
worked_examples.name: Cumulative risk pass (two Category 1 nitrosamines)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Cumulative risk rule: sum(measured / AI_limit) < 1.0 (FDA Rev 2); (20/26.5 + 20/26.5) ≈ 1.509 > 1.0 → fails
worked_examples.name: Cumulative risk fail (two Category 1 nitrosamines at high dose)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Cumulative risk rule: ratio must be < 1.0 (strict inequality); 26.5/26.5 = 1.0 is NOT < 1.0 → fails
worked_examples.name: Cumulative risk at limit (exactly 1.0 fails)

invariants.assertion_hint: For any valid call to classify_m7(): if result.m7_class == 5 and result.data_basis == 'qsar', then assert result.in_silico_concordance == 'concordant_negative'.
invariants.description: Every Class 5 assignment driven by QSAR decision (data_basis == 'qsar') must have both in-silico systems negative (in_silico_concordance == 'concordant_negative'). This enforces the dual-QSAR rule: positive from either system -> Class 3.
invariants.name: Class 5 (QSAR-driven) requires concordant negative

invariants.assertion_hint: For any valid call to classify_m7(): if result.m7_class == 1, then assert input experimental_carcinogen == 'positive'.
invariants.description: Every Class 1 assignment must be backed by positive experimental carcinogenicity data. This enforces ICH M7(R2) section 6 definition: Class 1 = known mutagenic carcinogen (mutagenicity + carcinogenicity data positive).
invariants.name: Class 1 requires experimental carcinogenicity data

invariants.assertion_hint: For any valid call to classify_m7(): if result.coc_flag == True and result.m7_class != 5, then assert result.ttc_ug_per_day is None.
invariants.description: Any Cohort of Concern compound (coc_flag == True) that is not Class 5 must have ttc_ug_per_day == None. This enforces ICH M7(R2) section 7.5: Cohort of Concern compounds require compound-specific acceptable intakes; the TTC does not apply to high-potency mutagenic carcinogens (N-nitroso, alkyl-azoxy, aflatoxin-like).
invariants.name: Cohort of Concern never permits TTC-based acceptable intake

invariants.assertion_hint: For duration_months ∈ [0.5, 1, 1.5, 6, 12, 24, 120, 121, 240], call classify_m7(alerting_structure, duration_months=d, experimental_*=None) and verify ttc_ug_per_day matches the correct band threshold and is monotonically decreasing (or stable at boundary).
invariants.description: The TTC value is strictly monotone decreasing with increasing exposure duration: ≤1mo(120) > >1-12mo(20) > >1-10yr(10) > >10yr(1.5). This reflects decreasing safe exposure levels over longer durations for a single mutagenic impurity (10^-5 risk).
invariants.name: Staged TTC is monotonically non-increasing in dose band

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 5 = no structural alert, or alerting structure with data demonstrating lack of mutagenicity; dual QSAR rule (both negative)
worked_examples.name: Class 5 (Concordant negative QSAR, no structural alert)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 3 = alerting structure, unrelated to drug substance, with no mutagenicity data; discordant QSAR treated as positive (Class 3)
worked_examples.name: Class 3 (Discordant QSAR with alerting structure)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 3 default when structural alert detected and no experimental override; staged TTC for >1-10 years (default duration=120mo)
worked_examples.name: Class 3 (Alerting structure, no in-silico data provided)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 2 = known mutagen with unknown carcinogenic potential (bacterial mutagenicity positive, no rodent carcinogenicity data); staged TTC applies
worked_examples.name: Class 2 (Experimental Ames positive)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 1 = known mutagenic carcinogen (mutagenicity + carcinogenicity data positive); requires compound-specific acceptable intake (not TTC)
worked_examples.name: Class 1 (Experimental carcinogenicity positive)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 5 achieved when negative carcinogenicity study overrides structural alert and positive QSAR; experimental data is determinative
worked_examples.name: Class 5 (Experimental carcinogenicity negative overrides alert)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 5 from negative bacterial assay (experimental Ames); experimental data overrides structural alert
worked_examples.name: Class 5 (Experimental Ames negative overrides alert)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 7.5 (Cohort of Concern): N-nitroso compounds are high-potency mutagenic carcinogens; TTC not applicable, compound-specific AI required
worked_examples.name: Class 2 (Cohort of Concern: N-nitroso, no carcinogenicity data)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6 & 7.5: Class 1 (mutagenic carcinogen) overrides CoC distinction when carcinogenicity data are positive
worked_examples.name: Class 1 (Cohort of Concern: N-nitroso with carcinogenicity positive)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 7.5: Cohort of Concern compounds are not cleared by negative bacterial assay; only negative carcinogenicity study clears them to Class 5
worked_examples.name: Class 2 (Cohort of Concern not cleared by negative Ames)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 7.5: Negative carcinogenicity study definitively clears a Cohort of Concern compound to Class 5
worked_examples.name: Class 5 (Cohort of Concern cleared by negative carcinogenicity)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) staged TTC: ≤1 month exposure → 120 microg/day
worked_examples.name: Staged TTC band: ≤1 month

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) staged TTC: >1 month to 12 months exposure → 20 microg/day
worked_examples.name: Staged TTC band: >1 month to 12 months

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) staged TTC: >1 to 10 years exposure → 10 microg/day
worked_examples.name: Staged TTC band: >1 to 10 years

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) staged TTC: >10 years to lifetime exposure → 1.5 microg/day (lifetime TTC)
worked_examples.name: Staged TTC band: >10 years to lifetime

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) section 6: Class 3 when both QSAR systems are concordant positive and no experimental data override; concordant results do not force expert review
worked_examples.name: Class 3 (Positive QSAR, either system)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH M7(R2) encoded rule-set (auditable, version-controlled); table reference: staged TTC (less-than-lifetime acceptable intakes)
worked_examples.name: m7_rule_set() returns encoded parameters

invariants.assertion_hint: For any result from calculate_q3ab_thresholds, assert that for all three thresholds (reporting, identification, qualification): effective_percent <= percent_rule + 1e-9 (floating-point tolerance)
invariants.description: The effective_percent (after resolving absolute caps) must never exceed the percent_rule from the guideline band, since effective_percent = min(percent_rule, cap_as_percent)
invariants.name: effective_percent_never_exceeds_percent_rule

invariants.assertion_hint: For each threshold: (absolute_is_binding and absolute_cap is not None) implies cap_as_percent < percent_rule; (not absolute_is_binding) implies (absolute_cap is None or cap_as_percent >= percent_rule)
invariants.description: When absolute_is_binding is True, the absolute cap must exist (not None) and the calculated cap_as_percent must be strictly lower than percent_rule; when False, either no cap exists or cap_as_percent >= percent_rule
invariants.name: absolute_is_binding_consistency

invariants.assertion_hint: For Q3A: if daily_dose_g <= 2.0, dose_band contains '≤ 2 g'; if > 2.0, contains '> 2 g'. For Q3B ID: if dose_mg < 1, band = '<1mg'; if [1,10], = '1-10mg'; if (10,2000], = '>10mg-2g'; if > 2000, = '>2g'. Qual bands: [0,10), [10,100], (100,2000], >2000
invariants.description: The dose_band label must match the actual daily_dose_g: for Q3A, ≤2g vs >2g; for Q3B, ID and qual bands must match the documented ranges (e.g., <1mg, 1-10mg, >10mg-2g, >2g)
invariants.name: dose_band_assignment_matches_dose_range

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH Q3B(R2) Attachment 1, band >2g
worked_examples.name: Q3B product 3g dose (>2g band, no absolute caps)

invariants.assertion_hint: For any solvent with class_number==1: assert pde_mg_per_day is None; assert concentration_limit_ppm is not None and > 0; verify that check_residual_solvent_limits with different doses yields same permitted_ppm
invariants.description: All Class 1 (avoid) solvents in the encoded table must have pde_mg_per_day=None and a positive concentration_limit_ppm that is dose-independent.
invariants.name: Class 1 solvents have no PDE and fixed concentration limit

invariants.assertion_hint: For any solvent with class_number==3: assert pde_mg_per_day == 50.0; assert concentration_limit_ppm == 5000.0
invariants.description: All Class 3 (low toxic potential) solvents must have pde_mg_per_day exactly 50.0 and concentration_limit_ppm = 5000.0 (50 * 100).
invariants.name: Class 3 solvents all share PDE = 50 mg/day

invariants.assertion_hint: For any solvent with class_number in (2,3): assert concentration_limit_ppm == approx(pde_mg_per_day * 100.0); verify across all 39 Class 2/3 entries
invariants.description: For Class 2 and Class 3 solvents, the reported concentration_limit_ppm (Option 1, at 10 g/day reference dose) must always equal the PDE scaled by 100.
invariants.name: Class 2/3 Option 1 identity: concentration_limit_ppm = pde_mg_per_day * 100

invariants.assertion_hint: For a Class 2 solvent (e.g., methanol PDE=30), call check_residual_solvent_limits with doses [0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 10.0] and same measured value; assert permitted_ppm values are [60000, 30000, 15000, 3000] respectively and inversely proportional
invariants.description: For Class 2/3 solvents, Option 2 permitted_ppm = PDE * 1000 / daily_dose_g increases monotonically as daily_dose_g decreases. Measured pass/fail is deterministic.
invariants.name: Option 2 dose-scaled limit scales monotonically with dose reduction

invariants.assertion_hint: For benzene (Class 1, 2 ppm limit): call check_residual_solvent_limits with doses 0.1, 1, 10 g/day, same measured value; assert all yield permitted_ppm == 2.0
invariants.description: Class 1 solvents use a fixed concentration limit regardless of daily_dose_g. Permitted ppm must remain constant.
invariants.name: Class 1 limit does not scale with dose

invariants.assertion_hint: For all ComplianceResult in output: assert margin_ppm == approx(permitted_ppm - measured_ppm); assert passed == (measured_ppm <= permitted_ppm) or passed is None for unknown solvents
invariants.description: For any compliance check result: margin_ppm = permitted_ppm - measured_ppm; passed = (measured_ppm <= permitted_ppm).
invariants.name: Margin equals permitted minus measured

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH Q3C(R8) Appendices 1-3, Class 2 Table
worked_examples.name: Lookup by alias (DMF resolves to N,N-Dimethylformamide)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH Q3C(R8) Option 2 (dose-scaled): permitted_ppm = PDE * 1000 / daily_dose_g = 30 * 1000 / 0.5 = 60000
worked_examples.name: Compliance Class 2 dose-scaled (Methanol 3000 ppm @ 0.5 g/day dose)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH Q3C(R8) Class 1 concentration limit (fixed, dose-independent)
worked_examples.name: Compliance Class 1 fixed limit (Benzene @ 0.1, 1, 10 g/day all use 2 ppm)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH Q3C(R8) Class 1 concentration limit (fixed, dose-independent)
worked_examples.name: Compliance Class 1 fail (Benzene 5 ppm measured vs 2 ppm limit)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: ICH Q3C(R8) Option 2 at reference dose (10 g/day): 4.1 * 1000 / 10 = 410 ppm (matches Option 1)
worked_examples.name: Compliance Option 2 == Option 1 at 10 g/day reference dose (Acetonitrile)

worked_examples.guideline_trace: Explicit unknown handling (not in ICH Q3C(R8) Appendices 1-3 subset)
worked_examples.name: Unknown solvent returns explicit unmapped state

invariants.assertion_hint: For all 72 test cases (24 elements × 3 encoded routes), assert get_element_pde(symbol, route).control_threshold_ug_per_day == pytest.approx(0.30 * get_element_pde(symbol, route).pde_ug_per_day)
invariants.description: For any encoded route, the control_threshold_ug_per_day must equal 0.30 times the pde_ug_per_day. This is a regulatory constant from ICH Q3D(R2) section 3.2.
invariants.name: Control threshold is exactly 30% of PDE

invariants.assertion_hint: For any element, route, and two doses d1 < d2, assert calculate_concentration_limit(element, route, d1).permitted_concentration_ppm * d1 == pytest.approx(calculate_concentration_limit(element, route, d2).permitted_concentration_ppm * d2), i.e. the product PDE / dose must be constant
invariants.description: For a fixed element and route, the permitted concentration in ppm is inversely proportional to the daily dose in grams. Doubling the daily dose must halve the concentration limit.
invariants.name: Permitted concentration scales inversely with dose

invariants.assertion_hint: Parse q3d_rule_set()['elements']; assert len(elements) == 24; assert all(e['element_class'] in ('1', '2A', '2B', '3') for e in elements); assert Counter([e['element_class'] for e in elements]) == {'1': 4, '2A': 3, '2B': 10, '3': 7}
invariants.description: All 24 Q3D elements must have exactly one of four class values ('1', '2A', '2B', '3') and the distribution must match: 4 Class 1, 3 Class 2A, 10 Class 2B, 7 Class 3. The class is the same across all routes for a given element.
invariants.name: Class classification is immutable and complete

invariants.assertion_hint: For each element and route, assert (route in ('oral', 'parenteral', 'inhalation')) == (get_element_pde(element, route).pde_ug_per_day is not None and get_element_pde(element, route).route_data_available is True)
invariants.description: For any element, get_element_pde returns pde_ug_per_day != None and route_data_available == True only if the route is in ('oral', 'parenteral', 'inhalation'). Cutaneous/transcutaneous always return None and route_data_available == False.
invariants.name: Only encoded routes return non-None PDE

invariants.assertion_hint: For any element and route, assert calculate_concentration_limit(element, route, 0.0) raises DataValidationError and calculate_concentration_limit(element, route, -1.0) raises DataValidationError
invariants.description: calculate_concentration_limit and risk_assessment_report must reject non-positive daily doses with DataValidationError.
invariants.name: Negative or zero dose is rejected

invariants.assertion_hint: assert get_element_pde('Fe', 'oral') raises DataValidationError; assert get_element_pde('iron', 'oral') raises DataValidationError; assert get_element_pde('unobtanium', 'oral') raises DataValidationError
invariants.description: get_element_pde must fail with DataValidationError if the element is not one of the 24 ICH Q3D-listed elements, whether looked up by symbol or name.
invariants.name: Unknown element lookup fails
